title: Fix output message for "podman start" command.
body:
Fix output message for "podman start" command: it prints short container ID instead of full one.

Signed-off-by: Boris Klimenko <2@borisklimenko.ru>

comments.body:
Hi @dasmfm. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Changes LGTM, I'll leave it to others to decide on the output change.
Tests aren't happy, but I don't think it's related to this PR.

>I'll leave it to others to decide on the output change.

To be clear - all the other actions with containers return full ID now, and only "podman start" with stopped container returns id from CLI. For example:

`root@das:~/repos# #container stopped

root@das:~/repos# podman start 9a575db48901

9a575db48901

root@das:~/repos# #container already started on previous step

root@das:~/repos# podman start 9a575db48901

9a575db48901410d333c4f844c3fa5cdc2257520e941c7b30de6955110ff95ae
`

Thanks for contributing, @dasmfm!

Can you elaborate _why_ this change is needed? The commit message states that this is a "fix" and I wonder if there's a bug. One way or another, the commit message should be more verbose and explain the motivation to perform the change, so future generations can understand what happened.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dasmfm*, *jwhonce* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@vrothberg I changed commit message, and I already described reasons here: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4270#issuecomment-542550680

thanks for the patch.  The code LGTM.

Could you please move the explanation to a new line in the commit message?

The first line should be a concise description of the change itself, with more details included in the body.  I'd suggest something like:

```
start: print full container ID

Now the "podman start" command prints the full ID and has the same
behaviour as other commands.
```
